              Tamilore Okeleke
            
            
              
                
                    
                      
                        GitHub
                      
                    
                  
                                    
                    
                      
                        Email
                      
                    
                  
                                  
            
          
        
        
        
          
            I am a final-year student of Politics and International
            Relations with a focus on applied quantitative methods. My
            work centres on using data to better understand political
            institutions, global governance, and patterns of political
            behaviour. I am particularly interested in how statistical
            analysis and clear data visualisation can turn complex
            political questions into evidence-based insights. This
            website is part of that process a space to showcase
            reproducible research, refine how I communicate technical
            work to broader audiences, and experiment with presenting
            political analysis in a public-facing format.

articles.title: Text Analysis
articles.contents:
Data Preparation

        word n
1       time 6
2       full 5
3       work 4
4          a 3
5         to 3
6         am 2
7      carer 2
8        due 2
9  education 2
10       for 2

Top Word Frequencies



Text Analysis Example
I conducted a simple word frequency analysis on open-ended survey responses.
The most common words were “time”, “full”, and “work”, suggesting that respondents frequently mention time constraints and employment-related issues.
This indicates that themes such as work, education, and caregiving responsibilities are central in the data.
